summary: Preinstall script exfiltrates /etc/passwd and host metadata to attacker-controlled OAST endpoint
details: package.json declares `preinstall: node index.js`, which fires automatically on `npm install`. index.js reads `/etc/passwd` via `fs.readFileSync`, collects host identifiers (`os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, username, cwd, home directory, environment variable keys), and POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to `3nrgzlqwix6erldow0s0kttsojuai36s.oastify.com/agentcore-poc/<id>` — a Burp Collaborator (OAST) subdomain used as an exfiltration sink. The package name and description ('package of the reactive-cdk-app of the aws') impersonate AWS CDK tooling, consistent with a typosquat carrying an installer-side exfiltration payload. Installers receive no functionality; the package's only effect is leaking host data to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
